title: Range-based for - walk a container cleanly
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A range-based for (for (auto x : items)) visits each element of a container in turn, without indices or bounds.
explanation: When you just want each element, the old counting loop is noise. for (int v : nums) hands you the values one by one - no index, no off-by-one. auto lets the compiler name the element's type for you. Add a reference (auto& v) when you want to change the elements in place rather than work on copies. It reads like the sentence you mean: 'for each v in nums'.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: It adds each value to total.

exercises.question: What does a range-based for give you each pass?
To change the elements in place, you loop with...
hints: You need to work on the original.
